summary:
The primary non-discharge criterion involves maintaining stable temperature without recurrent spikes; normalization of platelet count also remains critical post-DHF treatment before considering release. Appetite improvement by itself isn't definitive evidence justifying immediate discharge due to its limited correlation with full resolution necessary clinically speaking. To ensure safety during this phase, other indicators like hematological stability must be confirmed first. Agent1 concludes based solely upon medical guidelines provided within the context given about DHF management protocols regarding discharge considerations. [End] ```markdown
The main reason a patient cannot be discharged from Dengue Haemorrhagic Fever care includes lack of sustained normal body temperature and normalized platelet counts. While improved appetite indicates partial recovery, it doesn’t suffice independently for discharge decisions which require more robust signs of complete health stabilization.
```
